tiers.tier1: Common application patterns that form the primary production support target.
tiers.tier2: Advanced patterns that are supported when explicitly listed.
tiers.tier3: Experimental, planned, or explicitly unsupported behavior.

features.name: File-backed embedded databases
features.summary: UseCSharpDb supports direct embedded file-backed databases.

features.name: Private in-memory databases
features.summary: A private :memory: database is supported while its supplied connection remains open.

features.name: Remote transports, pooling, and named shared memory
features.summary: The provider rejects endpoint, non-direct, pooled, and named shared-memory configurations.

features.name: Composite primary keys and indexes
features.summary: Composite primary keys and ordered composite indexes are supported.

features.name: Foreign keys
features.summary: Named scalar and composite foreign keys support create, standalone add/drop, primary or alternate-key targets, and cascade or restrictive delete behavior.

features.name: Alternate keys and unique constraints
features.summary: Single and composite alternate keys are created as named unique constraints and support standalone add/drop migrations.

features.name: Literal column defaults
features.summary: Literal INTEGER, REAL, TEXT, BLOB, and NULL defaults are supported; DefaultValueSql is not.

features.name: Check constraints
features.summary: Create-table checks and standalone add/drop migrations are supported for deterministic row-local expressions accepted by the engine.

features.name: Computed columns
features.summary: Computed column metadata is rejected pending engine and catalog support.

features.name: Core CLR type mappings
features.summary: Boolean, integral, enum, floating-point, string, GUID, temporal, and byte-array values round-trip.

features.name: Exact decimal mapping
features.summary: Decimal and nullable decimal use provider-owned scaled INTEGER storage without an application converter. The default is decimal(18,2); precision 1-18 and scale 0-precision support exact round trips, parameters used with one facet mapping, comparisons, ordering, and ordinary indexes. Excess fractional digits and overflow are rejected rather than rounded. Keys, defaults, generated values, custom store types, computed decimal expressions (including arithmetic, numeric casts, conditionals/coalescing, and aggregates), decimal collection/subquery Contains, cross-facet parameter reuse, comparisons with application-converter decimal mappings, model-mapped decimal functions, and precision/scale-changing migrations remain unsupported and fail explicitly. Complex properties are rejected until flattened mappings are qualified.

features.name: EnsureCreated
features.summary: EnsureCreated supports file-backed and private in-memory databases.

features.name: Database.Migrate and migration history
features.summary: Compiled migrations apply to file-backed databases and can be safely rerun.

features.name: Non-idempotent migration scripts
features.summary: IMigrator generates executable SQL for the supported migration operation set.

features.name: Idempotent migration scripts
features.summary: Conditional migration-history blocks can be generated and rerun against empty, partial, and current databases.

features.name: Standalone primary-key migrations
features.summary: Named single-TEXT and composite INTEGER/TEXT logical primary keys support standalone add/drop migrations. A physical single-INTEGER primary key can also be added to populated data: validated non-NULL unique values become the physical row IDs while ready ordinary/unique SQL, constraint-owned, and foreign-key-support indexes are rebuilt atomically. Full-text, collection, and non-ready indexes remain unsupported. EF drops require the exact constraint name, raw ALTER TABLE DROP PRIMARY KEY remains available for legacy unnamed keys, and dropping a key preserves NOT NULL.

features.name: Numeric column type changes
features.summary: AlterColumn supports dependency-free INTEGER-to-REAL and REAL-to-INTEGER shadow-root rewrites. INTEGER values must be exactly representable as REAL; REAL values must be finite, integral, and within Int64 range. TEXT/BLOB conversions and columns used by keys, indexes, or foreign keys remain unsupported. A view on the table, a table-owned trigger, a cross-table trigger that references the column, or an applicable validation rule must be removed first.

features.name: Text column collation changes
features.summary: AlterColumn supports TEXT collation changes through an atomic table-and-index shadow-root rewrite, including return to the default BINARY collation. Ready ordinary and unique SQL indexes that inherit the column collation are rebuilt; explicit-collation and unrelated indexes retain their roots. Checks and uniqueness are revalidated, and a failure restores every original root. Key constraints, foreign keys, full-text/collection dependencies, views on the table, table-owned triggers, cross-table triggers that reference the column, and applicable validation rules remain unsupported.

features.name: Practical schema alterations
features.summary: Add, drop, and rename columns; table rename; create/drop/rename index operations; named check, unique, foreign-key, and bounded primary-key add/drop; populated single-INTEGER primary-key rekeying with relational-index rebuilding; AlterColumn literal-default/nullability changes; dependency-free exact INTEGER/REAL rewrites; and TEXT-collation rewrites with inherited ordinary/unique SQL-index rebuilding are supported. Broader primary-key rekeys, indexed numeric changes, key/FK/full-text/collection-dependent column rewrites, and TEXT/BLOB type conversions remain unsupported.

features.name: Basic LINQ queries
features.summary: Where, ordering, Skip/Take pagination, scalar projection, Single, Any, Count, non-decimal constant/parameter collection Contains, and simple Include queries are supported.

features.name: Bounded direct inner joins
features.summary: One explicit Queryable.Join between sources that normalize to direct mapped entity roots supports a filtered outer source, one direct nonnullable INTEGER-backed int, long, or int/long-backed enum key per side, qualified scalar or entity result projections, and post-join filtering, ordering, and Skip/Take pagination. Integer-key behavior is cross-checked with SQLite, and enum, long-key, and self-join aliases are covered. Filtered inner sources, prior ordering or row limits, source shapes that remain projected or derived after EF normalization, nullable/text/decimal/configured-converter/transformed/composite keys, custom comparers, chained joins, GroupJoin, SelectMany, DefaultIfEmpty, and left/right joins are rejected before command dispatch with CDBEF1007 or CDBEF1003.

features.name: String members and methods
features.summary: String Length, parameterless ToLower/ToLowerInvariant, ToUpper/ToUpperInvariant, Trim/TrimStart/TrimEnd, Replace(string, string), and both Substring overloads translate in predicates, projections, and ordering. LOWER/UPPER use invariant CSharpDB server semantics rather than CurrentCulture.

features.name: Date and time components
features.summary: DateTime Year/Month/Day/Hour/Minute/Second, DateOnly Year/Month/Day, and TimeOnly Hour/Minute/Second translate in predicates and projections.

features.name: Double-precision math functions
features.summary: For finite REAL-mapped values, Math.Abs(double), Round(double), Floor(double), Ceiling(double), Truncate(double), and Sign(double) translate in predicates, projections, and ordering with SQL null propagation. Round uses midpoint-to-even semantics. Integral, decimal, MathF, precision-argument, midpoint-mode, and transcendental overloads remain outside the qualified surface.

features.name: Scalar numeric aggregates
features.summary: Count, LongCount, simple and bounded-shape Any, Sum over int/double/nullable double, Average over double/nullable double, and Min/Max over int/double/nullable double are qualified for filtered, empty, and all-null scalar queries. Long- and float-valued Sum/Average/Min/Max variants, integer Average, text Min/Max, and other unqualified aggregate types and shapes remain outside the qualified surface.

features.name: Scalar distinct numeric aggregates
features.summary: An optional Where followed by selection of one directly mapped nonnullable int column and Distinct supports Count, LongCount, Sum, Min, and Max. Distinct Average, nullable or non-int columns, value-converted columns, ordering, limits, predicates after Distinct, computed or composite selectors, casts, intervening operators, and derived sources are rejected with CDBEF1004 before command dispatch. Nullable distinct Count/LongCount cannot preserve LINQ's rule that NULL is counted once because SQL COUNT(DISTINCT) ignores NULL.

features.name: Grouped numeric aggregates
features.summary: Direct single-table GroupBy supports optional pre-filtering; direct mapped bool, integral, enum, default-BINARY string, and nullable keys; C# anonymous-type or ValueTuple composite keys; one projection containing direct keys with bare Count/LongCount, qualified non-distinct Sum/Average/Min/Max, and nonnullable-int direct-column Distinct Count/LongCount/Sum/Min/Max; basic HAVING including aggregate IS NULL; and ordering by a directly projected key or aggregate. Boolean keys require canonical provider-written 0/1 storage. Double, transformed, non-BINARY-collated, or configured-converter keys; aggregate value converters; Distinct Average; element/result selector overloads; group materialization; raw or post-projection group transforms; nested or joined grouping; predicate/CASE aggregates; casts; and broader types or shapes are rejected with CDBEF1005 before command dispatch.

features.name: Unsupported LINQ diagnostics
features.summary: Unsupported CLR methods and members, recognized TakeWhile/SkipWhile, Concat/Union/Except/Intersect, Join(comparer), GroupJoin/SelectMany/DefaultIfEmpty/LeftJoin/RightJoin, and ExecuteUpdate operators, unsupported distinct/grouped/inner-join shapes, and unsafe decimal expressions retain EF Core's InvalidOperationException and add CDBEF1001-CDBEF1007 guidance before command dispatch.

features.name: Advanced LINQ translation
features.summary: String StartsWith/EndsWith/instance Contains and StringComparison overloads, DateTimeOffset components, broader distinct and grouped aggregate types and shapes, set operations, composite/chained/outer/cross or derived-source joins, and correlated-query coverage remain planned.

features.name: CRUD and change tracking
features.summary: Tracked insert, update, delete, generated integer keys, and affected-row checks are supported.

features.name: Application-managed concurrency tokens
features.summary: Original concurrency-token values participate in write predicates and stale writes throw DbUpdateConcurrencyException.

features.name: Database-generated rowversion
features.summary: IsRowVersion and Timestamp semantics are rejected pending database-generated token support.

features.name: EF Core design-time migrations
features.summary: The provider registers design-time services for migrations add, script, and database update workflows.
